source_paper_title: Tightness Without Counterexamples: A New Approach and New Results for Prophet Inequalities
context:
Section 1.2 (New Results), item 2, page 6

It remains a challenging problem to analytically verify for all k > 1 our numerical observation that lim_{n\to\infty} \(\gamma_{k,n}\) = inf_{n\ge 1} \(\gamma_{k,n}\), which would resolve the open question of k-unit IID prophet inequality.
title: Prove that for each fixed number of slots \(k>1\), the worst-case IID \(k\)-unit prophet-inequality ratio equals its large-\(n\) limit
latex:
Given integers \(k\ge 2\) and \(n\ge 1\), consider the IID \(k\)-unit prophet inequality problem with \(n\) sequentially arriving agents. Let \(R_1,\dots,R_n\) be i.i.d. nonnegative random variables with known distribution \(F\). An online policy \(\pi\) observes \(R_i\) upon arrival of agent \(i\) and must irrevocably decide whether to accept it, subject to accepting at most \(k\) agents in total. Let
\[
\mathrm{ALG}_\pi(F;n,k):=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{i\in A_\pi} R_i\Big]
\]
be the expected reward of \(\pi\), where \(A_\pi\subseteq\{1,\dots,n\}\) is the random set of accepted indices with \(|A_\pi|\le k\). Let the prophet benchmark be
\[
\mathrm{PROPH}(F;n,k):=\mathbb{E}\Big[\sum_{t=1}^k R_{(t)}\Big],
\]
where \(R_{(1)}\ge \cdots \ge R_{(n)}\) are the order statistics of \(R_1,\dots,R_n\).

Define the optimal online value
\[
\mathrm{DP}(F;n,k):=\sup_{\pi}\ \mathrm{ALG}_\pi(F;n,k),
\]
where the supremum is over all (possibly randomized) online policies \(\pi\) that are measurable with respect to the observed history.

Define the worst-case competitive ratio for fixed \((n,k)\) in the IID setting by
\[
\gamma_{k,n}:=\inf_{F}\ \frac{\mathrm{DP}(F;n,k)}{\mathrm{PROPH}(F;n,k)},
\]
where the infimum is over all distributions \(F\) on \([0,\infty)\) with finite \(\mathrm{PROPH}(F;n,k)\) and \(\mathrm{PROPH}(F;n,k)>0\).

Problem: Prove that for every fixed integer \(k\ge 2\),
\[
\lim_{n\to\infty} \gamma_{k,n} \,=\, \inf_{n\ge 1} \gamma_{k,n}.
\]
Equivalently, show that the worst-case IID \(k\)-unit prophet-inequality ratio over all finite horizons \(n\) is attained (in the limit) as \(n\to\infty\).
open_problem_quote: It remains a challenging problem to analytically verify for all k > 1 our numerical observation that lim_{n\to\infty} \(\gamma_{k,n}\) = inf_{n\ge 1} \(\gamma_{k,n}\), which would resolve the open question of k-unit IID prophet inequality.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
